query: "Renewable energy defies Trump's attacks, reaching a new record" Los Angeles Times
answer: U.S. renewable energy reached a record high in 2025, generating 26% of the nation's electricity despite Trump administration policies against it. Renewable energy surpassed fossil fuels in production. The trend continues with new capacity plans.

results.title: Renewable energy defies Trump's attacks, reaching a new record
results.snippet: U.S. utilities generated a record amount of energy from renewable sources last year, even as the Trump administration implemented a range of policies to stymie green energy. Although it was a good year for renewables, the surge in energy demand from data centers, electric vehicles and industry also boosted power generated from fossil fuels. Nearly 80% of the power plant capacity planned to be added over roughly the next decade is tied to renewable sources, according to filings with federal regulators and grid operators compiled by Cleanview.co, an energy data company. The government estimates that 93% of new generation capacity expected to be added to the grid this year will come from wind, solar and batteries. results.title: POLITICO Pro | Article | Renewable energy defies Trump’s attacks, reaching new record
results.snippet: You have **undefined articles remaining** that you can gift this month. While several large natural gas plants opened in the U.S. last year, their capacity was eclipsed by a wave of smaller renewable facilities that came online. **ENERGYWIRE |** U.S. utilities generated a record amount of energy from renewable sources last year, even as the Trump administration implemented a range of policies to stymie green energy. Some 1,162 terrawatt-hours of the country’s electricity was generated from renewable sources in 2025, a 10 percent increase over the prior year, according to federal data released this week. That represents 26 percent of all U.S. electricity made — enough to power about 108 million U.S. homes for a year. Some months were even greener; in March, for example, renewables generated nearly 1 in 3 of the country’s electrons. That stands in contrast to the Trump administration slashing incentives for wind and solar while gutting clean air regulations in a bid to help fossil fuels.
